lesson.menu_desc: Directions and Asking for Help -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Getting Help in the City -- Directions and Asking for Help
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Dienane Brandão de Mesquita | Aula 14 -- Getting Help in the City

listenings.text: I am in a new part of the city, and I am lost. I want to find the bank, but I do not know the way. I see a man on the street, so I ask him. Excuse me, how do I get to the bank? He says, go straight for two blocks, then turn left at the traffic light. The bank is on your right. I say thank you, and I follow his directions. I go straight, I cross the street, and I turn left. The bank is right there, on my right. Now I am not lost.

Hello, this is a message about the way to our office. From the bus stop, go straight for one block. Then turn right at the traffic light and cross the street. Walk straight, and our building is on your left, next to the pharmacy. Do not turn left at the first corner, because that is the wrong way. If you are lost, call me and I will help you. See you soon at the office.

extra_audio.text: First, go straight for two blocks. Then turn left at the traffic light. Next, cross the street. After that, walk a little more. Finally, the bank is on your right.
